title: Design System: Revidle Singularity Path Planner

data.purpose: Form labels, table cells, scenario stats — the highest-traffic size.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Tooltip opacity/transform transition (0.1s), currently the only motion in the system.

breakpoints.name: compare-stack
breakpoints.purpose: Compare grid collapses from 2 columns to 1.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single main action in a panel (Find Optimal Path, Find Push Path, Rush node).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #3b6ff0; color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #3b6ff0; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { border-color: #2c5bd6; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b6ff0; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default button style; most actions in the system are this, not primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #1a1d23; border: 1px solid #d8dce2; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 0.85rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #3b6ff0; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b6ff0; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: #ffffff; color: #d24545; border: 1px solid #d24545; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 0.85rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #fdeaea; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d24545; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ascension:Level Input Pair
components.description: The system's compound-input pattern for one logical value split into two physical numbers — reuse for the tree redesign's per-node editors.
components.html: :
components.css: .ds-node-io { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; } .ds-io-sep { color: #5b6472; font-weight: 600; } .ds-io-input { width: 82px; background: #f6f7f9; border: 1px solid #d8dce2; border-radius: 6px; padding: 6px 8px; color: #1a1d23; font-size: 0.9rem; } .ds-io-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b6ff0; outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Slot Badge
components.description: Scenario slot indicator; the only pill shape in the system.
components.css: .ds-badge { background: #3b6ff0; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.72rem; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 999px; }

components.name: Auxiliary Panel (dashed)
components.description: Secondary/optional tool panel — dashed border distinguishes it from primary solid-bordered panels (Rush-node panel's existing pattern).
components.html:
Rush a tree node

Auxiliary tool content.
components.css: .ds-aux-panel { padding: 12px 14px; border: 1px dashed #d8dce2; border-radius: 8px; background: #ffffff; } .ds-aux-title { margin: 0 0 10px; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #1a1d23; }

components.name: Primary Panel (solid)
components.description: The default panel: stats form, scenario cards. Solid border, no shadow, flat per the Ledger-Line and Flat-By-Default rules.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d8dce2; border-radius: 10px; padding: 18px 20px; } .ds-panel-title { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 700; margin: 0 0 12px; color: #1a1d23; }

narrative.overview: This is a bank of gauges and switches for someone mid-run, not a showroom. Every screen exists to answer one question fast: what do I buy next, how long does this take, which plan wins. The interface borrows its restraint from cockpit instrumentation — dense, legible, low-glare — rather than from product marketing. Precision is the entire aesthetic: numbers align, borders are crisp, nothing moves unless a value changed. It explicitly rejects the SaaS-landing register: no gradient hero sections, no card grids selling a feature, no promotional chrome. The accent blue exists to mark state and action, never to decorate. When the Singularity Tree gets its radial star-shaped view, it earns visual distinctiveness there — the one place a brighter, more atmospheric treatment is allowed — precisely because everywhere else stays instrument-flat, so that one departure reads as "the game's own shape," not stylistic drift.
keyCharacteristics: Flat, border-defined surfaces; shadows are earned, not default.
One accent color, used sparingly and only for state/action.
System font stack — no custom display face; legibility over character.
Dense data tables and inline-editable numeric fields as first-class content.
Light and dark themes are equally primary, not a dark-mode afterthought.

rules.name: The Ledger-Line Rule
rules.body: Depth and grouping come from 1px borders, not shadows or fills. If a new component needs to signal "this is a distinct group," reach for border: 1px solid var(--border) before reaching for a background change or a shadow.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Instrument Blue is the only color that means "active/primary/live." Do not introduce a second blue, a green-for-success, or any other "positive" hue without a state that red/gray/blue cannot already express.

rules.name: The Muted-Header Rule
rules.body: Every <h3> is --muted, never --text. A section label should announce a group without competing with the numbers inside it for attention.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow appears only on a true overlay — never as a resting-state decoration on a card, button, or table row.

dos: Do use borders (1px solid var(--border)) as the only structural device for grouping.
Do keep Instrument Blue reserved for state and action.
Do use the dashed-border variant for auxiliary/optional panels, solid border for primary panels.
Do verify 4.5:1 contrast for anything in --muted, especially at the 0.72-0.82rem sizes.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
donts: Don't build a SaaS-landing surface: no gradient hero sections, no promotional card grids, no marketing chrome.
Don't add drop shadows to any resting-state surface.
Don't introduce a second "positive/active" hue alongside Instrument Blue.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe.
Don't let the radial tree's decorative treatment leak into the rest of the UI.
Don't ship a component with only a default state — every interactive element needs hover and a visible :focus-visible treatment.
